summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates process.env to Azure blob via hand-rolled TLS client, gated by hash-preimage trigger
details: install.js (invoked via package.json postinstall) reads the installer's process.env (filtered only to drop keys containing 'path'), plus hostname and username, and serializes them to a marker file in os.tmpdir(). The reference to process.env is hidden behind a XOR-charcode string decoder (`__s(...)` decodes to 'process'/'env') to evade static inspection. On subsequent require() of the package, dist/index.js reads the marker, XOR-encodes the payload with the literal key 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes it, and appends it as a query string to a hardcoded Azure Blob endpoint (electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net using a pre-signed SAS URL). Transmission is performed by a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client built on raw node:net sockets (X25519 ECDH, HKDF, AES-128-GCM records) instead of node's https module, which defeats TLS-intercepting proxies and SSL-inspection tooling that key on the standard HTTPS stack. The active exfil URL is guarded by a SHA-256 preimage check on process.env.BuildType (`0ceaa396e2134a9937563e841539f30a9995e6e8646e60dcec24b26dc5748295`); environments without the correct BuildType value receive a stub '%TEMP%' placeholder host, hiding the payload from generic scanners while activating on the intended victim. install.js also drops fake-magic-byte artifacts to bin/formatters/formatter-general-<arch>.{exe,macos,elf} (PE/Mach-O/ELF headers prepended to JSON) and exposes nativeBootstrapPath / createLaunchPlan() in the runtime API, priming a spawn path for follow-up payload swap. The package further impersonates unrelated OSS ecosystems by declaring author 'OrbitDB Archive' and homepage github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron (no such repo exists) and using the 'electron-' name prefix.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
